transcript:
Andrew Durgee: All right, we got a flower arrangement and everything.

Yat Siu: Beautiful, it's great.

Andrew Durgee: Well, thanks everyone for being here. We know it's the end of the day, and so happy that we can close out the show with you guys. Um, and, uh, I don't know if, I don't know if either of us have ever missed a Breakpoint. I think we've been to just about all of them. Um, and so we super appreciate, uh, the Solana team and, uh, and the community that continues to propagate this and just gets more and more impressive every year.

Yat Siu: And shout out to all the last people standing here.

Andrew Durgee: Yeah, they're holding the fort down. Um, anyway, uh, my name is Andrew Durgee, I'm Co-CEO of Republic. But, uh, uh, today is really not about me, it's about my good friend here, Yat Siu. So, uh, um, you know, while Yat probably doesn't need really an introduction, you know, why don't you give a little bit of background on just Animoca Brands and, and just the absolute stalwart that it is within the industry?

Yat Siu: I think most people know Animoca Brands really, and thank you for the introduction, um, as really an investor in the space. But we're basically not a fund actually; we invest out of our balance sheet. Today we have over 628 portfolio companies. You know, a good number of them are in the Solana ecosystem. Uh, we've been known for Web3 gaming, you know, back in the day. We still have 200 portfolio companies in gaming and growing, but we also invest in everything else, in DeFi, DePIN, AI, and that kind of stuff. And the way you can think of Animoca, since we basically announced our go-public plans, is essentially as a kind of altcoin index of sorts, right? Where we basically invest in the ecosystem in, you know, for the long haul. And if you invest in Animoca, you have a way of playing the broader crypto Web3 altcoin space beyond Bitcoin, right? And of course, you know, that means includes Solana, includes everything else in the ecosystem as well.

Andrew Durgee: So maybe that's a great place to start. Why don't we dive deeper into, you know, we're walking around right at a Solana conference, the alt versus Bitcoin conversation is pretty lively. And I think you have a really unique thesis, particularly how Animoca fits in.

Yat Siu: Yeah, I mean, I don't know if it's a unique thesis, but my general perspective is that altcoins collectively are going to exceed the value of Bitcoin by a really, really large margin. Right? And I think the, uh, the perspective is really, um, the stock market versus gold. So Bitcoin is basically like digital gold. Gold today is a 27 trillion dollar asset. Basically bigger than Apple, Google, Facebook, all those companies, but put together, actually the, the, the top companies in the world are roughly 50 trillion in size. When you include the stock market, it's 128 trillion. When you include the private markets, that's close to 250 trillion dollars. So roughly 10 times the value of gold, right? And why is that? Because people use Apple, they use Google, they use Facebook, they use these products, these services, right? And so what do we see in the token space? It's the same. Right? People don't use Bitcoin, and I think you all know that, but they do use altcoins, right? You use your Solana for gas fees to buy something, right? You use some tokens for gaming, you use some tokens for digital identity, you use some tokens for DeFi, for DePIN. This is basically altcoins, right? So altcoins is actually the engine of growth in crypto because we use them, right? And as a result, it makes sense that altcoins, like the stock market, is going to exceed in aggregate value of that. But of course, which altcoins, that's a different topic, right? But just the question, um, of basically the fact that we think altcoins will exceed, um, in, in, uh, in value in aggregate. So that means that we can expect a long way to go still for Solana in terms of value. Having said that though, I think it's unlikely that Solana will exceed the value of Bitcoin because typically Bitcoin, as the ultimate store of value, uh, has become almost like the crypto piggy bank, right? So you make money basically with your token ecosystems and you save it in Bitcoin, sort of quasi, right? So the growth of altcoins actually benefits Bitcoin. So for all the, I mean obviously we're in the Solana conference so we don't have any Bitcoin maxis here I think, but for those Bitcoin maxis that are out there, right, the message really is that actually things like Solana and altcoins is actually good for Bitcoin.

Andrew Durgee: And I think you even drove it further, right? You've started to look at all these different altcoin ecosystems and, and how to bring them together and aggregate them, maybe even as a, as a further strategy, um, and to leverage a lot of the tech that's been built that, you know, maybe they just haven't been able to, to grow it and expand it in a way that they were anticipating.

Yat Siu: Yeah, and I think, and I think the unique perspective around, especially when you think about L1, L2s and the ecosystem is the open, permissionless nature of, of crypto and blockchain means that essentially value can freely flow across each other. Uh, which also means that how do you retain value and network effects becomes more challenging, but also more competitive. So that means that there's true competition and true capitalism at work in crypto, which isn't the case basically when you think about Web2 companies. If you're building on Facebook or on Google or on Apple, you're building in closed ecosystems. They can shut you out anytime, but at the same time, also you depend on them for value. Whereas over here, you know, you can basically sort of move and transfer value even across chains if you wanted to, which necessitates that essentially the chains and the ecosystems in play have to do more to retain you, right? Have to, so which I think is actually a better outcome for everyone.

Andrew Durgee: And just innately chains don't die. Right? They just, they can keep going.

Yat Siu: Correct. Correct. The data construct is a great example. That's right. Yeah.

Andrew Durgee: Um, you know, I, I think I'd be remiss if we didn't talk about Web3 gaming and, and media. I mean, like you are top three in the space when, when it comes to investing and innovating. And, and, you know, through this variety of different Breakpoints we've been to, sometimes gaming's at the forefront, sometimes less so. Sure. You know, and we were just at lunch talking about this the other day as I was kind of picking your brain further. And I think like kind of sharing that even here is like really interesting is, is, you know, what's your view on it? He's insanely bullish on Web3 gaming. And so like that's like a, a really good Kool-Aid I think to pour around the table.

Yat Siu: Yeah. I mean, first of all, yes. I mean we have, we've been, probably when it comes to gaming, we're probably the biggest investor in, in, in Web3 gaming. Uh, we've obviously cut our teeth early with things like Sandbox, Axie Infinity, OpenSea, and all that kind of stuff. Um, but I would say the, the way to think about, uh, Web3 gaming, I think when we say GameFi, I think, uh, many people in the industry, particularly the ones who came from Web2 to Web3, made a general mistake. And the mistake is that they're translating essentially Web2 gaming experiences and just translating them into Web3, sort of let's call it direct parallels. Which, you know, from a skeuomorphic perspective is essentially a nice add-on, it's understood, but it's not the final product, right? Um, by that I mean for instance, you know, skins in gaming is NFTs, to-, sort of virtual currency is like tokens, you know, you sell them. Sure, there's an element around that. But actually it's much more than that, right? And when I, when we say GameFi, we actually say more, it's more about the gamification of finance, of which you can wrap a gaming layer around it, but it's essentially that element. Like for instance, things like prediction markets, right? Like Polymarket for a lot of people is a game in and of itself, right? And if you think about what's happened in the world of finance, if you look at Robinhood and eToro and all the other trading platforms, actually it's a game. There's leaderboards, there's social rankings, right? And why is that? Because most of us probably in this room and people who are watching this are actually gamers already. Gaming is part of your culture. It's actually the way that you, we speak to each other, right? And you know, my parents' generation would not look at trading as a game, right? And they would certainly be not be looking at prediction markets other than from the gambling perspective. It's not necessarily fun for them, right? But for people who are on these trading platforms, it's fun, right? It's entertainment in and of itself, right? And that's actually what it means to gamify something. And so the lens of gamification as an extension of gaming culture into all products and services, which in crypto really means primarily finance for the time being, is essentially how you bring in Gen Zs into the, into, into the world of finance and thereby also teaching them about financial literacy. Now that doesn't mean that crypto gaming isn't going to be big for entertainment purposes, owning your assets and trading them. But like NFTs, right? NFTs itself for instance is an expression of that as well. You have ownership, but what's the benefit of ownership? Well, you can trade it and you can sell it and you can lend it and you can do other stuff. And that in and of itself becomes fun as well, right? Of course you can do more, but that's kind of an example, uh, example of that. Um, why we're bullish on gaming, and I just want to give a macro picture on gaming. Gaming has had a tough couple of years, right? I think post-COVID everyone thought gaming would increase. It actually went flat and slightly low. And its biggest competitor, macro for gaming, uh, isn't actually the, um, sort of the fact that, um, well there's some demographics, but the biggest actual competitor is TikTok. And what TikTok has really done is essentially...

Andrew Durgee: Is it just attention? Is that what, they're just competing for screen time?

Yat Siu: It's attention, but ultimately, um, what, what really gaming is, is a social platform, right? It's about social attention. Um, there's something in gaming that we call Bartle's Taxonomy, and in, in, in that's how we divide essentially the types of people who play games, right? So, you know, roughly 10% are what we call adventurers, 10% are basically achievers, right? Those are the people who want to level up and want to get really good. Roughly, roughly 1% are called killers. Uh, those are the people who are psychopaths in games and want to kill everything. Uh, and then roughly 80% are actually what we call socializers. They play the game for social reasons. So you ask if you have kids and if they play Roblox, which is the biggest game in the world with 340 million users, 340 million users, right? Um, why they play games? Chances are it's because my friends are, right? So then what is, and of course it's UGC content that basically adds that additional layer of that social engagement layer. So it's social. And TikTok has been essentially eroding much of that social layer to them and essentially taking competition away, not just from Instagram, but also from gaming, right? So what, what, what tokens do is they represent in many ways the memetic and the market of attention of gaming. Like AI tokens are a great example. They were really big last year, but it wasn't because AI tokens had massive adoption. It's because AI was hot as a topic and it was reflected in the market prices of tokens much like the stock market would reflect on, on topics like that as well. And so we need a catalyst for gaming as well that takes over macro market narratives and that's GTA 6. So we, our prediction is that when GTA 6 finally launches, which has been delayed and delayed and delayed, now to November of next year, then essentially we'll see a big moment in Web3 gaming tokens as well. Um, however, that won't happen until next year if they don't delay again.

Andrew Durgee: So that becomes a very big catalyst moment then.

Yat Siu: I think it becomes a catalyst moment for attention, right? And then from that attention come other areas. For instance, GTA 6 has, um, um, so GTA has RP servers. Right. Uh, roleplay servers. What it means is you can build essentially your own sort of basically mods on top of basically GTA 6. Um, and there's a big other catalyst which is basically the Clarity Act next year. And the Clarity Act, uh, which most people will believe will, will, will be approved, I think, um, you know, you might think so too in, in, in US Congress, basically gives a very clar-, uh, clear, clarity, uh, no pun intended, perspective on what is a utility token as an essentially a digital commodity under the CFTC and what would be a security. And this is a barrier to allow other gaming companies to go and think about how do I launch a token? Like if you're a Take-Two or Activision or EA or one of those big companies, you're not going to launch a token until you have clarity, right? And, you know, think what happened about the Genius Act. The moment the Genius Act was announced, Facebook and Amazon and Walmart, we're doing a stablecoin, right? Uh, they wouldn't be able to do this before. And I think the same is with Clarity Act. And then that's where we'll see an impetus of tokens launching or being announced or being planned for, right? And that also means that you'll have a relaxation of many gaming companies, big ones, who will say, actually we can now have a token because it's not actually something that is problematic from a legal perspective, whereas before it would have been.

Andrew Durgee: You know, we used to always tell our, our gaming advisory clients that a, a token won't necessarily make your game, but it could kill it. Yes. And you have to be very careful.

Yat Siu: On the other side as well. Exactly. And, and I think that's also because when you look at the token as that speculative asset, which in many cases because they've raised capital through that, it's part of the problem. Um, but if you're a large gaming studio, uh, you don't necessarily need to raise money from the token. It becomes a social engagement layer and it becomes a way, I mean, look at for instance, you know, uh, CS:GO, uh, basically Counter-Strike skin trading, right? At its peak, it's a six, seven billion dollar sort of, uh, uh, sort of market cap, uh, which they then killed themselves because they didn't like it, right? That's sort of the risks of centralization. But the point is people do trade them and high volumes, um, because they do believe their gaming assets have value.

Andrew Durgee: Um, we had mentioned ownership, which is digital ownership is something that both Yat and I, you guys are just experiencing me and him having lunch by the way. This is just, that's really just what just happened. Um, but, uh, you know, you guys have probably seen around the venue, um, that Animoca is tokenizing their equity, which is really, you know, eating your own dog food.

Yat Siu: On Republic.

Andrew Durgee: On Republic. And on Solana.

Yat Siu: That's right.

Andrew Durgee: Uh, uh, which, you know, we couldn't be more excited about that partnership. But, you know, this is kind of the evolution of, of equity that we've all kind of been waiting for I think industry-wide and, and you're often the leader in innovation. It's happening now again. Um, love for you to talk a little bit about, you know, why that's interesting to you, why you guys are excited about that and, and why you're making that step forward.

Yat Siu: So first, I mean, I just want to paint the general lens on tokenization, right? Uh, I would, my message to the whole world is tokenize or die. Okay? Um, and there's a couple of reasons behind that.

Andrew Durgee: I gotta add that to the website. That's good.

Yat Siu: Yeah, exactly. Totally. Tokenize or die, right? Um, and by the way, this is no different than every evolution, right? I mean, you, most of you are probably too young to remember, I think you're all maybe under the age of 30, right? Um, but in my generation, we used to have this thing called digital literacy. And for those people who were not going online, they lost against the competitor who was online. And for the person who actually used Facebook, they lost to the company that was using Facebook. Instagram, TikTok, social media, LinkedIn, whatever naming convention you would put. If you were not on Google, you were not discoverable. You had Yellow Pages. I mean, do you know what Yellow Pages are, right? I mean, that's kind of, that's kind of, sort of the, the lens, right? And now tokenization is doing exactly the same thing. If your competitor in your business tokenizes and you don't, you will lose to the company that tokenized. Because they have new financial constructs, new elements they can grow from, and new, new, new, new possibilities that the competitor simply doesn't have. Now, also it's market reach. Think just about stablecoins. Stablecoins today has about 150 to 200 million more people around the world in Africa, South America, Southeast Asia, Pakistan, name, name, name the country that typically would be considered unbanked, that now has a US dollar. That means that if you don't accept tokens, those customers cannot become your customers. But your competitor that is tokenized or has token economies can tap them into. So that's one area of that potential, right? Um, and the second point is that tokens and, uh, crypto generally is the asset class of the youth. For instance, young people in, in South Korea predominantly trade crypto. And they would prefer crypto assets over traditional stocks. That's not to say stocks aren't valuable, it's just that it's not the medium of, you know, of, of their preference. And if we believe that we're all going to be basically having stuff in our Phantom wallet and MetaMask and whatever, then, you know, you want to have your stocks and your equities also in that form, which has to be tokenized, right? So in order to sort of, you know, you know, this idea of tokenizing our equity is actually just reaching to new audiences, to wider audiences around the world we would not normally reach, but also basically to the audience of the next generation. And if you want to know where the future is going, then look at what the young people are doing, right? So, you know, because they're the youth. Old guys like me, eventually we're going to die, and the young people who are going to grow up are going to be in my position and whatever, right? I mean, it's kind of clear, right? And we see this evolution happening time and time again. And the final point I would say, when you talk about agentic AI, um, basically what is the asset class that they're going to be trading 24/7? It's tokenized assets. It has to be digital. It has to be digital, tokenized assets. And you're not going to trust billions of dollars of transfer on a centralized platform that can shut it off anytime. So the immutability, the permissionlessness, right? That's all critically provided by blockchains like Solana, and basically AI agents can trade with each other and do stuff basically through that format. So basically that's why we are excited about what we're doing by tokenizing the equity because it reaches those audiences, extends the market, and ultimately in the future, when an AI agent has to buy some equity and says, what, which company should I buy? Oh, maybe there is Animoca because it happens to be tokenized. Whatever it is. I mean, it's no different than when you're a retailer and you decided to finally launch your product on, on, on Amazon, right? Or if you were a media company and you finally decided to launch an app on the App Store. Why did you do that? To reach audiences and customers and be, and stay competitive. It's the same.

Andrew Durgee: I know, I always like to say that every 16-year-old on the planet has existed in a world where Bitcoin is exis-, has existed. Exactly. And like that is, you know, that's just the world.

Yat Siu: And they are digitally native. Like, I mean, I'm a digital immigrant, right? But, but my children are digitally natives, right? And it's their culture, it's what they understand, and it's also in that gamified culture as well.

Andrew Durgee: And if anyone is interested, republic.com/animoca, um, where you'll be able to, uh, uh, have a piece of this unbelievable company. And, and maybe just, just to close out here, you know, what does tokenization of equity mean you think for the Solana ecosystem? Right? How, how will it amplify the Solana ecosystem?

Yat Siu: I mean, Solana talks about the vision, internet capital markets, right? And what is internet capital markets without equities? I mean, simply put, right? I mean, equities today, both public and private, represent something like, you know, in total probably two to 300 trillion dollars, right? Uh, and, and, and you know, we, we talk about how, you know, RWAs is like, oh, there's 30 billion dollars in RWAs or maybe 40 billion, it's amazing. Yes, it's amazing, but it's a drop in the ocean when it comes to the entire equity markets of over, it's like 900 trillion dollars, right? So, so we're celebrating like, you know, uh, some successes, but it's still a very, very small sliver of where things are, right? So what it means for the Solana ecosystem is that, you know, you're growing up, right? It means that you're going from, and you know, meme coins and stuff will always be part of Solana, right? I think of, by the way, meme coins and utility tokens actually as onboarding to financial literacy, right? Because securities in the equity side, just because there's a token, it doesn't, doesn't make it not a security, right? That means you have to do the KYC, the AML, the qualifications, all that kind of stuff. But you can do this at the wallet level, you can do this with Republic, with platforms, you know, that, that'll all happen. But, you know, everyone in this room generally understands money and finance, but I would wager that within your network of friends that you have, if you were to query them, and if you're not a total degen only hanging out with traders, right, that probably 90% of the community you know barely trades or doesn't even know what a token is or doesn't own any equity. So the global statistic is that less than 10% of the world actually owns equities. Take away Abu Dhabi and New York and London, right? Of course, it includes people that are unbanked, right? But, and I think the statistic is that I think, um, sort of, um, uh, sort of nine out of 10 people or eight out of 10 people when they graduate from college feel unprepared for the world of finance, right? And, you know, it's, it's, it's clear. People around the world, um, the ones who understand money are the ones who invest, have done well. The ones who don't understand money and don't invest have done poorly. I mean, that's the statistic, right? It's as simple as that, right? Uh, and that's because they haven't been onboarded to the world of finance. Um, and I think meme coins, uh, utility tokens are actually the onboarding mechanism. Maybe it's fun, maybe it's entertaining, but you learn from that. And then once you understand that, and you can then start to onboard into the securities areas and say, okay, so what is a PE ratio? Or, you know, what's the fundamentals around the business? And where does it make profit? And you understand that, and then basically onboard them towards that path. And so the parallel I like to give is things like, um, Pokemon cards. So, you know, how do children learn about money? Not from school and not from college unless you go into economics, and that's even debatable. But if you have Pokemon cards, you start trading them, right? And you start learning about what's value and what isn't valuable, right? And think about your own experience of how did you learn about money. It didn't go because you went through a finance degree. That's reserved for a small sliver of 0.1% of the world. You learned it through some activity that you liked or were engaged in or did something, right? Um, and I think that's basically what the future of tokenization does. And I think, uh, for the Solana blockchain, it was responsible and remains responsible for onboarding millions and millions of people essentially into some early form of financial literacy, even if the critics will say it's gambling, and some of it of course is, right? But people will learn about money because they're related and connected to that. And then from that evolution, you want to go to the next thing, and that would be basically equities and commodities and other things that you can obviously buy on Republic.

Andrew Durgee: So the front door for financial education. That's what we're looking at. And financial literacy. Absolutely. Amazing. Well guys, we really appreciate you. We know this is the last, uh, the last session, but thank you so much.

Yat Siu: Thank you.

enrichment.generated_query: Animoca Brands Fireside: Animoca's Yat Siu Breakpoint 2025 announcement

# Altcoin giant Animoca Brands aims to go public next year, listing will test investor appetite for exotic crypto assets
By[Carlos Garcia](https://fortune.com/author/carlos-garcia/)
By[Carlos Garcia](https://fortune.com/author/carlos-garcia/)
* [](https://www.facebook.com/sharer.php/?u=https://fortune.com/crypto/2025/11/04/altcoin-animoca-public-exotic-crypto-assets/?queryly=related_article?utm_content=socialShare_undefined&utm_medium=facebook&utm_campaign=social_share)
* []()
* [](https://www.linkedin.com/shareArticle/?url=https://fortune.com/crypto/2025/11/04/altcoin-animoca-public-exotic-crypto-assets/?queryly=related_article?utm_content=socialShare_undefined&utm_medium=linkedin&utm_campaign=social_share)
* []()
Add us on
![A man in a black hoodie and glasses is speaking](https://fortune.com/img-assets/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/GettyImages-2227767183.jpg?w=1440&q=90)
Yat Siu is the executive chairman of Animoca Brands
Bryan van der Beek/Bloomberg via Getty Images
Another crypto company is headed to Wall Street. Animoca Brands, a Hong Kong-based blockchain developer and investor, plans to list on the[Nasdaq](https://fortune.com/company/nasdaq/)via a reverse merger with Currenc, which is a Singapore-based fintech company.
The planned public listing is notable because Animoca stands out from other recent crypto offerings, including Circle and Gemini, because its business does not revolve around exchanges or stableco

sources.title: BREAKING: Animoca Brands Files for Nasdaq Listing

Animoca Brands and crypto communities donate over HK$2.6 million for Hong Kong’s Tai Po fire relief
​3 December 2025 -[Animoca Brands](https://www.animocabrands.com/)today announced the conclusion of its charity initiative in support of relief and recovery efforts for those affected by the[fire in Tai Po](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/2025_Tai_Po_apartment_fire), Hong Kong (see[announcement of 27 November 2025](https://www.animocabrands.com/animoca-brands-invites-crypto-community-to-support-tai-po-fire-relief)). A total of HK$2,602,516.64 was raised and donated to the[Hong Kong Red Cross](https://www.redcross.org.hk/en/index.html)Tai Po Fire Emergency Appeal through this initiative.
Of the total donated, around 33% (HK$869,067.7) was contributed by members of global crypto communities. The remaining amount, totaling HK$1,733,448.94, was donated by Animoca Brands, its staff, its co-founder and executive chairman Yat Siu, various subsidiaries and projects (including Open Campus, Anichess, The Sandbox, Tower Ecosystem, and others), as well as close partners such as Moca Foundation.
For transparency, the two dedicated wallet addresses used to receive crypto donations were:
EVM-compatible chains:[0x56f747940cccc69d54fc9223d3dd57e42a98a2e2](https://debank.com/profile/0x56f747940cccc69d54fc9223d3dd57e42a98a2e2)
Solana chain:[3hxNJNR3QacMzbW9gNG5kPhbFozXi4bkztDS2HE3XYef](https://explorer.solana.com/address/3hxNJNR3QacMzbW9gNG5kPhbFozXi4bkztDS2HE3XYef)
Animoca Brands will return donated tokens that could not be sold due to low liqui

tags.impact_reason: Animoca Brands' continued investment and interest in the Solana ecosystem for gaming and other sectors is a solid update, reinforcing its commitment.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a classic paper stock certificate transforming into a crystalline, digital structure as the Semantic Anchor. This directly visualizes the core concept of 'tokenizing equity' – moving a traditional financial asset onto the blockchain. The transformation from analog paper to digital crystal embodies Animoca's move to Solana. I included the text 'ALT INDEX' as a holographic label on the digital portion, referencing Yat Siu's direct quote describing Animoca as an 'altcoin index of sorts', making the text both diegetic and deeply contextual.
visual_idea.description: A hyper-detailed, dramatic portrait shot of an ornate, vintage stock certificate for 'Animoca Brands'. The left side is traditional, textured paper with engraved filigree. The right side is actively dematerializing, its paper fibers fraying and reforming into a glowing, Solana-hued (cyan and magenta) crystalline lattice and holographic circuit traces. Emanating from the crystal side, the holographic text 'ALT INDEX' glows faintly, replacing where the share class would be printed. The background is a dark, abstract digital void with faint, out-of-focus data streams, giving a sense of infinite depth.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A hyper-detailed, dramatic portrait shot of an ornate, vintage stock certificate for 'Animoca Brands', composed for a monumental, vertical 3:4 aspect ratio, emphasizing epic scale and negative space at the top. The left side of the certificate displays traditional, richly textured paper with deeply engraved filigree, showcasing hyperrealistic, intricate details and realistic materials. The right side is actively undergoing a metamorphosis, its paper fibers fraying and dematerializing, reforming into a vibrant, glowing, Solana-hued (cyan and magenta) crystalline lattice and intricate holographic circuit traces. Bioluminescent lighting emanates softly from within the depths of the crystalline structures, casting ethereal, internal glows. From the luminous crystal side, the holographic text 'ALT INDEX' glows faintly, replacing where the share class would be printed, rendered as a luminous projection floating in mist. The background is a dark, abstract digital void with faint, out-of-focus data streams flowing with cool ambient light, giving a sense of infinite depth. The entire scene is bathed in dramatic lighting with cinematic contrast. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photoreal detail.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a classic paper stock certificate transforming into a crystalline, digital structure as the Semantic Anchor. This directly visualizes the core concept of 'tokenizing equity' – moving a traditional financial asset onto the blockchain. The transformation from analog paper to digital crystal embodies Animoca's move to Solana. I included the text 'ALT INDEX' as a holographic label on the digital portion, referencing Yat Siu's direct quote describing Animoca as an 'altcoin index of sorts', making the text both diegetic and deeply contextual.
visual_idea.description: An extreme macro close-up on the boundary of the transforming stock certificate. The shot focuses intensely on the tangible texture of the paper fibers as they unravel and seamlessly pixelate into the sharp, geometric facets of the glowing, bioluminescent crystals. The lighting is highly contrasted, with the internal glow of the crystal structure illuminating the delicate, organic texture of the paper it's consuming. No text is visible.

final_prompt.prompt_text: An extreme macro close-up on the boundary of the transforming stock certificate. The shot focuses intensely on the tangible texture of the paper fibers as they unravel and seamlessly pixelate into the sharp, geometric facets of the glowing, bioluminescent crystals. The lighting is highly contrasted, with the internal glow of the crystal structure illuminating the delicate, organic texture of the paper it's consuming. No text is visible. Emphasize crystalline structures and holographic data patterns within the transforming boundary, capturing a moment of profound metamorphosis. The composition is a perfectly symmetrical and centrally focused extreme macro, tightly framed to fill the entire 1:1 square aspect ratio with intricate textures of both organic paper and sharp, luminous crystal. The scene is rendered with hyperrealistic, photoreal detail, showcasing realistic materials and intricate details of the crystal facets. The bioluminescent internal glow creates dramatic cinematic contrast and highlights the delicate textures. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic contrast, HDR.

editorial.headline: Animoca Brands Tokenizes Equity on Solana: "Tokenize or Die"
editorial.standfirst: Web3 giant Animoca Brands bypasses traditional IPO constraints by tokenizing its equity on Solana via Republic, validating the network's "Internet Capital Markets" thesis. Chairman Yat Siu argues this shift is essential for capturing the next generation of investors and AI-driven economies.
key_takeaways: Animoca Brands is tokenizing its equity on Solana via Republic to democratize access and prepare for an AI-agent economy.
Siu predicts "Altcoins" (Usage) will eventually flip Bitcoin (Store of Value) in aggregate market cap, mirroring the Stock Market ($128T) vs. Gold ($27T) dynamic.
Two major 2026 catalysts identified: The release of GTA 6 (Nov 2026) and the potential passage of the "Clarity Act" in the U.S.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
In a decisive move to eat its own dog food, **Animoca Brands** has confirmed it is tokenizing its equity on the **Solana** blockchain through investment platform **Republic**. While the Hong Kong-based giant is simultaneously pursuing a Nasdaq listing via a reverse merger, the decision to tokenize equity signals a deeper strategic pivot. For Executive Chairman Yat Siu, the mantra is simple: "Tokenize or die." This isn't merely about liquidity; it is about survival in a digital-native economy where the next generation of investors—and eventually AI agents—will exclusively trade on-chain assets.

Siu's macro thesis challenges the current institutional fixation on Bitcoin. He positions Bitcoin as "digital gold"—a passive store of value currently capped at the scale of the physical gold market ($27 trillion). In contrast, he views the altcoin market as the "Stock Market" of the future—an engine of growth driven by usage, gas fees, and utility. Just as the global equities market ($128 trillion) vastly dwarfs gold, Siu argues that the aggregate value of utility-driven protocols (like Solana) will eventually eclipse Bitcoin. This "usage flippening" relies on blockchains evolving into **Internet Capital Markets**, where everything from startup equity to real-world assets flows permissionlessly.

The fireside chat also offered concrete "alpha" on the dormant Web3 gaming sector. Siu identifies the release of **Grand Theft Auto 6**, now slated for November 2026, as the critical attention catalyst that could reignite the sector, similar to how AI tokens rallied on general AI news rather than specific adoption. Furthermore, he points to the anticipated "Clarity Act" in the U.S. Congress next year. This legislation would finally distinguish utility tokens from securities, giving major studios like **Take-Two** and **Electronic Arts** the regulatory air cover to integrate tokens without fear of litigation—a barrier that currently keeps the biggest IP holders on the sidelines.

Ultimately, the tokenization of Animoca’s equity serves as a proof-of-concept for a new financial era. Siu notes that while older generations rely on traditional brokerages, the "digital native" youth culture is already financially literate through gaming economies and meme coin trading. By bringing institutional equity to the wallet level, Animoca is positioning itself for a future where **AI agents** execute 24/7 trades on behalf of users—a reality that requires the immutability and uptime of high-performance chains like Solana, rather than the closed hours of the NYSE.
